title: Steer: inspect + kill-by-PID stop with /stop command
description: Implement the steer paths. Inspect summarizes the disk roster (already available via /runs and the classifier steer:inspect action). Stop performs a real hard-stop: RunSpawner.stop resolves the PID from the session pid sidecar, calls process.kill, and flips the run's disk state.json status to 'aborted' via writeRunState; when the PID is unknown it falls back to the disk 'aborted' flip only and reports fallbackFlagOnly. Add a deterministic `/stop <runId>` slash-command and wire the classifier steer:stop action. The target runId is resolved against the disk roster at stop-time (never from spawn-time memory).

successCriteria.description: The project builds successfully with zero TypeScript errors.
TypeScript compilation produces zero type errors.
All tests pass with zero failures, including new collocated tests for this sprint.

RunSpawner.stop(runId) resolves the PID from the pid sidecar, calls the (injected) kill function with that PID, and flips state.json status to 'aborted' via writeRunState; a unit test asserts both the kill call with the recorded PID and the on-disk status change to 'aborted'.
When the PID is unknown (no sidecar entry), stop does NOT call kill on any pid, flips the disk status to 'aborted' only, and returns StopResult with fallbackFlagOnly:true; covered by a unit test.
A deterministic `/stop <runId>` slash-command invokes RunSpawner.stop without an LLM call; a unit test feeds '/stop run-x' to a session whose injected LLMClient throws if called and asserts stop ran.

The classifier steer:stop action routes to RunSpawner.stop, and the target runId is resolved against the current disk roster at stop-time (a unit test asserts a runId not present in the roster yields a 'no such running run' reply rather than killing anything).
The classifier steer:inspect action and `/runs` both return a roster summary; a unit test asserts the steer:inspect path returns the same summary RosterReader.summarize produces.
process.kill is only ever called with a PID recorded by this session lifetime; a unit test confirms an unknown/stale runId never reaches the kill function (guards against killing arbitrary PIDs).

nonGoals: Do not implement child-side cooperative abort polling (the child may not observe the disk flag in Phase 1)
Do not implement pause/resume or A2A input-required states (Phase 2)
Do not use RunManager.abortRun for cross-process stop (it no-ops; run-manager.ts:123)
Do not kill PIDs not recorded by this session
stopConditions: Spawning a detached run and then issuing /stop <runId> terminates the child process and the roster shows the run as 'aborted'
definitionOfDone: RunSpawner.stop(runId, reason) resolves the PID from the session pid sidecar, calls process.kill, and flips the disk state.json to 'aborted'; unknown PID falls back to the disk flip only with fallbackFlagOnly:true. A deterministic /stop <runId> command and the classifier steer:stop action both route here, resolving the runId against the disk roster at stop-time. steer:inspect and /runs return the roster summary. process.kill is restricted to session-recorded PIDs. Build, typecheck, and all tests pass.
assumptions: process.kill is acceptable for a real hard-stop of the detached child in Phase 1; cooperative child shutdown is deferred
writeRunState is the primitive for the stop-time 'aborted' status flip (src/state/run-state.ts)
The pid sidecar from Sprint 2 (.bober/chat/<sessionId>.pids.json) is the authoritative source of session-recorded PIDs
outOfScope: Killing runs not spawned by this session
generatorNotes:
Extend src/chat/run-spawner.ts with stop(runId, reason): 
1. Resolve the entry from the PidSidecar (src/chat/pid-sidecar.ts). 
2. If found: call the injected kill fn (default process.kill) with entry.pid; then writeRunState(projectRoot, {...existing, status:'aborted'}) — read current state via readRunState (run-state.ts:61) first, then write the flip. Return {stopped:true, runId, killedPid:entry.pid}.
3. If NOT found: do not call kill; read+writeRunState status 'aborted' if the run exists on disk; return {stopped:true, runId, fallbackFlagOnly:true} (or stopped:false if the run isn't on disk at all).

Dependency-inject the kill function so tests never kill real processes. Guard: only call kill when a sidecar entry exists (sc-4-9).

ChatSession: 
- /stop <runId> slash-command => RosterReader.read, confirm the runId is a running run, call RunSpawner.stop; reply with the StopResult. No LLM call.
- classifier {action:'steer',op:'stop',runId} => same resolution path (resolve against disk roster at stop-time; if not running, reply 'no such running run' and do NOT kill).
- classifier {action:'steer',op:'inspect'} and /runs => RosterReader.summarize(await RosterReader.read()).

Keep NL stop and slash /stop sharing one internal stop handler so behavior is identical.
evaluatorNotes:
sc-4-4: spawn (or pre-seed a pid sidecar entry + a running state.json), call stop with an injected kill capturing the pid; assert kill got the recorded pid and state.json is now 'aborted'.
sc-4-5: stop a runId with a state.json but no sidecar entry; assert kill NOT called, status 'aborted', fallbackFlagOnly true.
sc-4-6: feed '/stop run-x' to a session whose LLMClient throws on call; assert the internal stop handler ran.
sc-4-7: classifier returns steer:stop for a runId absent from the roster; assert reply indicates no such running run and kill not called.
sc-4-8: steer:inspect reply equals RosterReader.summarize output for the same states.
sc-4-9: unknown/stale runId path never reaches the injected kill fn.
Run build, typecheck, test. Optionally an integration test spawning a trivial detached sleep-like child and killing it, if feasible without flakiness.
